query:
You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.50 (commit: b139c14d).

## Issue to Resolve

Mutable collection property name should not be SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE
<!-- The bug you're experiencing might have already been reported!
Please search in the [issues](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues) before creating one. -->

## Expected Behavior
Mutable collection property name should not be SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE - https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide#non-constant_names
```kotlint
object : DumObjectName {
    val someMap: MutableMap<SomeKey, SomeValue> = ...
}
```

## Observed Behavior
ktlint require SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE:
```kotlint
object : DumObjectName {
    val SOME_MAP: MutableMap<SomeKey, SomeValue> = ...
}
```

## Steps to Reproduce
Create object, with mutable collection property. 

## Your Environment
<!--- Include as many relevant details about the environment you experienced the bug in -->
* Version of ktlint used: 0.50.0

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

You're right. Can you provide a little more context where this variable is declared? Is it at top level, or in a companion object or class?
I have edited the issue, so you can see where the property is declared. 

In my case it is an object's property. I did not check for other cases as top level or companion object's variables (SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE naming should not be applied to class properties according to https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide#constant_names).

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `documentation/snapshot/docs/rules/experimental.md`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/PropertyNamingRule.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

content.thinking:
Let me analyze this issue. The problem is that ktlint is requiring SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE for mutable collection properties in objects, but according to the Android Kotlin style guide, mutable collections should not use SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE naming.

Let me start by reading the relevant files to understand the current implementation.

conversation_history.name: read
conversation_history.content:
1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.CLASS_BODY
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.CONST_KEYWORD
  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.FILE
  6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.GET_KEYWORD
  7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.IDENTIFIER
  8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.MODIFIER_LIST
  9→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.OBJECT_DECLARATION
 10→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.OVERRIDE_KEYWORD
 11→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.PRIVATE_KEYWORD
 12→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.PROPERTY
 13→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.PROPERTY_ACCESSOR
 14→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.VAL_KEYWORD
 15→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule
 16→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
 17→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.children
 18→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
 19→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules.internal.regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters
 20→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 21→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.tree.IElementType
 22→
 23→/**
 24→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#function-names
 25→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#property-names
 26→ */
 27→public class PropertyNamingRule :
 28→    StandardRule("property-naming"),
 29→    Rule.Experimental {
 30→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 31→        node: ASTNode,
 32→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 33→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 34→    ) {
 35→        node
 36→            .takeIf { node.elementType == PROPERTY }
 37→            ?.let { property -> visitProperty(property, emit) }
 38→    }
 39→
 40→    private fun visitProperty(
 41→        property: ASTNode,
 42→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 43→    ) {
 44→        property
 45→            .findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
 46→            ?.let { identifier ->
 47→                when {
 48→                    property.hasCustomGetter() -> {
 49→                        // Can not reliably determine whether the value is immutable or not
 50→                    }
 51→                    property.isBackingProperty() -> {
 52→                        visitBackingProperty(identifier, emit)
 53→                    }
 54→                    property.hasConstModifier() ||
 55→                        property.isTopLevelValue() ||
 56→                        property.isObjectValue() -> {
 57→                        visitConstProperty(identifier, emit)
 58→                    }
 59→                    else -> {
 60→                        visitNonConstProperty(identifier, emit)
 61→                    }
 62→                }
 63→            }
 64→    }
 65→
 66→    private fun visitBackingProperty(
 67→        identifier: ASTNode,
 68→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 69→    ) {
 70→        identifier
 71→            .text
 72→            .takeUnless { it.matches(BACKING_PROPERTY_LOWER_CAMEL_CASE_REGEXP) }
 73→            ?.let {
 74→                emit(identifier.startOffset, "Backing property name should start with underscore followed by lower camel case", false)
 75→            }
 76→    }
 77→
 78→    private fun visitConstProperty(
 79→        identifier: ASTNode,
 80→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 81→    ) {
 82→        identifier
 83→            .text
 84→            .takeUnless {
 85→                // Allow
 86→                // object Foo {
 87→                //     private const val serialVersionUID: Long = 123
 88→                // }
 89→                it == SERIAL_VERSION_UID_PROPERTY_NAME
 90→            }?.takeUnless { it.matches(SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE_REGEXP) }
 91→            ?.let {
 92→                emit(
 93→                    identifier.startOffset,
 94→                    "Property name should use the screaming snake case notation when the value can not be changed",
 95→                    false,
 96→                )
 97→            }
 98→    }
 99→
100→    private fun visitNonConstProperty(
101→        identifier: ASTNode,
102→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
103→    ) {
104→        identifier
105→            .text
106→            .takeUnless { it.matches(LOWER_CAMEL_CASE_REGEXP) }
107→            ?.let {
108→                emit(identifier.startOffset, "Property name should start with a lowercase letter and use camel case", false)
109→            }
110→    }
111→
112→    private fun ASTNode.hasCustomGetter() = findChildByType(PROPERTY_ACCESSOR)?.findChildByType(GET_KEYWORD) != null
113→
114→    private fun ASTNode.hasConstModifier() = hasModifier(CONST_KEYWORD)
115→
116→    private fun ASTNode.hasModifier(iElementType: IElementType) =
117→        findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)
118→            ?.children()
119→            .orEmpty()
120→            .any { it.elementType == iElementType }
121→
122→    private fun ASTNode.isTopLevelValue() = treeParent.elementType == FILE && containsValKeyword()
123→
124→    private fun ASTNode.containsValKeyword() = children().any { it.elementType == VAL_KEYWORD }
125→
126→    private fun ASTNode.isObjectValue() =
127→        treeParent.elementType == CLASS_BODY &&
128→            treeParent?.treeParent?.elementType == OBJECT_DECLARATION &&
129→            containsValKeyword() &&
130→            !hasModifier(OVERRIDE_KEYWORD)
131→
132→    private fun ASTNode.isBackingProperty() =
133→        takeIf { hasModifier(PRIVATE_KEYWORD) }
134→            ?.findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
135→            ?.takeIf { it.text.startsWith("_") }
136→            ?.let { identifier ->
137→                this.hasPublicProperty(identifier.text.removePrefix("_"))
138→            }
139→            ?: false
140→
141→    private fun ASTNode.hasPublicProperty(identifier: String) =
142→        treeParent
143→            .children()
144→            .filter { it.elementType == PROPERTY }
145→            .mapNotNull { it.findChildByType(IDENTIFIER) }
146→            .any { it.text == identifier }
147→
148→    private companion object {
149→        val LOWER_CAMEL_CASE_REGEXP = "[a-z][a-zA-Z0-9]*".regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters()
150→        val SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE_REGEXP = "[A-Z][_A-Z0-9]*".regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters()
151→        val BACKING_PROPERTY_LOWER_CAMEL_CASE_REGEXP = "_[a-z][a-zA-Z0-9]*".regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters()
152→        const val SERIAL_VERSION_UID_PROPERTY_NAME = "serialVersionUID"
153→    }
154→}
155→
156→public val PROPERTY_NAMING_RULE_ID: RuleId = PropertyNamingRule().ruleId
157→

1→!!! important
   2→    Up and until Ktlint version `0.47`, experimental were located in a separate `experimental` rule set. As of Ktlint version `0.48`, each rule set can optionally define experimental rules.
   3→
   4→All experimental rules described below are part of the `standard` rule set of Ktlint. To enable all experimental rules (from all rule sets), set `editorconfig` property below:
   5→```ini
   6→[*.{kt,kts}]
   7→ktlint_experimental=enabled
   8→```
   9→Also see [enable/disable specific rules](../configuration-ktlint/#disabled-rules).
  10→
  11→## Binary expression wrapping
  12→
  13→Wraps binary expression at the operator reference whenever the binary expression does not fit on the line. In case the binary expression is nested, the expression is evaluated from outside to inside. If the left and right hand sides of the binary expression, after wrapping, fit on a single line then the inner binary expressions will not be wrapped. If one or both inner binary expression still do not fit on a single after wrapping of the outer binary expression, then each of those inner binary expressions will be wrapped.
  14→
  15→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
  16→
  17→    ```kotlin
  18→    fun foo() {
  19→        // Assume that the last allowed character is
  20→        // at the X character on the right                       X
  21→        if ((leftHandSideExpression && rightHandSideExpression) ||
  22→            (leftHandSideLongExpression &&
  23→                rightHandSideLongExpression)) {
  24→            // do something
  25→        }
  26→    }
  27→    ```
  28→
  29→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
  30→
  31→    ```kotlin
  32→    fun foo() {
  33→        // Assume that the last allowed character is
  34→        // at the X character on the right                       X
  35→        if ((leftHandSideExpression && rightHandSideExpression) || (leftHandSideLongExpression && rightHandSideLongExpression)) {
  36→            // do something
  37→        }
  38→    }
  39→    ```
  40→
  41→Rule id: `binary-expression-wrapping` (`standard` rule set)
  42→
  43→## Blank line before declarations
  44→
  45→Requires a blank line before any class or function declaration. No blank line is required between the class signature and the first declaration in the class. In a similar way, a blank line is required before any list of top level or class properties. No blank line is required before local properties or between consecutive properties.
  46→
  47→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
  48→
  49→    ```kotlin
  50→    const val foo1 = "foo1"
  51→
  52→    class FooBar {
  53→        val foo2 = "foo2"
  54→        val foo3 = "foo3"
  55→
  56→        fun bar1() {
  57→           val foo4 = "foo4"
  58→           val foo5 = "foo5"
  59→        }
  60→
  61→        fun bar2() = "bar"
  62→
  63→        val foo6 = "foo3"
  64→        val foo7 = "foo4"
  65→
  66→        enum class Foo {}
  67→    }
  68→    ```
  69→
  70→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
  71→
  72→    ```kotlin
  73→    const val foo1 = "foo1"
  74→    class FooBar {
  75→        val foo2 = "foo2"
  76→        val foo3 = "foo3"
  77→        fun bar1() {
  78→           val foo4 = "foo4"
  79→           val foo5 = "foo5"
  80→        }
  81→        fun bar2() = "bar"
  82→        val foo6 = "foo3"
  83→        val foo7 = "foo4"
  84→        enum class Foo {}
  85→    }
  86→    ```
  87→
  88→Rule id: `blank-line-before-declaration` (`standard` rule set)
  89→
  90→!!! Note
  91→    This rule is only run when `ktlint_code_style` is set to `ktlint_official` or when the rule is enabled explicitly.
  92→
  93→## Class signature
  94→
  95→Rewrites the class signature to a consistent format respecting the `.editorconfig` property `max_line_length` if set. In the `ktlint_official` code style all class parameters are wrapped by default. Set `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_class_signature_wrapping_rule_always_with_minimum_parameters` to a value greater than 1 to allow class with a few parameters to be placed on a single line.
  96→The other code styles allow an infinite amount of parameters on the same line (as long as the `max_line_length` is not exceeded) unless `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_class_signature_wrapping_rule_always_with_minimum_parameters` is set explicitly.
  97→
  98→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint (ktlint_official)"
  99→
 100→    ```kotlin
 101→    // Assume that max_line_length is not exceeded when written as single line
 102→    class Foo0
 103→    class Foo1(
 104→        a: Any
 105→    )
 106→    class Foo2(
 107→        a: Any,
 108→        b: Any
 109→    )
 110→    class Foo3(
 111→        @Foo a: Any,
 112→        b: Any,
 113→        c: Any
 114→    )
 115→    class Foo4(
 116→        a: Any,
 117→        b: Any,
 118→        c: Any
 119→    ) : FooBar(a, c)
 120→    class Foo5 :
 121→        FooBar(
 122→            "bar1",
 123→            "bar2",
 124→        ) {
 125→        // body
 126→    }
 127→    class Foo6(
 128→        val bar1: Bar,
 129→        val bar2: Bar
 130→    ) : FooBar(
 131→            bar1,
 132→            bar2
 133→        ) {
 134→        // body
 135→    }
 136→    class Foo7(
 137→        val bar1: Bar,
 138→        val bar2: Bar
 139→    ) : FooBar(
 140→            bar1,
 141→            bar2
 142→        ),
 143→        BarFoo1,
 144→        BarFoo2 {
 145→        // body
 146→    }
 147→    class Foo8
 148→        constructor(
 149→            val bar1: Bar,
 150→            val bar2: Bar,
 151→        ) : FooBar(bar1, bar2),
 152→            BarFoo1,
 153→            BarFoo2 {
 154→        // body
 155→    }
 156→    ```
 157→
 158→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed (ktlint_official)"
 159→
 160→    ```kotlin
 161→    // Assume that max_line_length is not exceeded when written as single line
 162→    class Foo0()
 163→    class Foo1(a: Any)
 164→    class Foo2(a: Any, b: Any)
 165→    class Foo3(@Foo a: Any, b: Any, c: Any)
 166→    class Foo4(a: Any, b: Any, c: Any) : FooBar(a, c)
 167→    class Foo5 : FooBar(
 168→        "bar1",
 169→        "bar2",
 170→    ) {
 171→        // body
 172→    }
 173→    class Foo6(
 174→        val bar1: Bar,
 175→        val bar2: Bar
 176→    ) : FooBar(
 177→        bar1,
 178→        bar2
 179→    ) {
 180→        // body
 181→    }
 182→    class Foo7(
 183→        val bar1: Bar,
 184→        val bar2: Bar
 185→    ) : FooBar(
 186→        bar1,
 187→        bar2
 188→    ),
 189→        BarFoo1,
 190→        BarFoo2 {
 191→        // body
 192→    }
 193→    class Foo8
 194→    constructor(
 195→        val bar1: Bar,
 196→        val bar2: Bar,
 197→    ) : FooBar(bar1, bar2),
 198→        BarFoo1,
 199→        BarFoo2 {
 200→        // body
 201→    }
 202→    ```
 203→
 204→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint (non ktlint_official)"
 205→
 206→    ```kotlin
 207→    // Assume that the last allowed character is
 208→    // at the X character on the right           X
 209→    class Foo0
 210→    class Foo1(
 211→        a: Any
 212→    )
 213→    class Foo2(a: Any)
 214→    class Foo3(
 215→        a: Any,
 216→        b: Any
 217→    )
 218→    class Foo4(a: Any, b: Any)
 219→    class Foo5(@Foo a: Any, b: Any, c: Any)
 220→    class Foo6(a: Any, b: Any, c: Any) :
 221→        FooBar(a, c)
 222→    class Foo7 : FooBar(
 223→        "bar1",
 224→        "bar2",
 225→    ) {
 226→        // body
 227→    }
 228→    class Foo8(
 229→        val bar1: Bar,
 230→        val bar2: Bar
 231→    ) : FooBar(
 232→        bar1,
 233→        bar2
 234→    ) {
 235→        // body
 236→    }
 237→    class Foo9(
 238→        val bar1: Bar,
 239→        val bar2: Bar
 240→    ) : FooBar(
 241→        bar1,
 242→        bar2
 243→    ),
 244→        BarFoo1,
 245→        BarFoo2 {
 246→        // body
 247→    }
 248→    class Foo10
 249→    constructor(
 250→        val bar1: Bar,
 251→        val bar2: Bar,
 252→    ) : FooBar(bar1, bar2),
 253→        BarFoo1,
 254→        BarFoo2 {
 255→        // body
 256→    }
 257→    ```
 258→
 259→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed (non ktlint_official)"
 260→
 261→    ```kotlin
 262→    // Assume that the last allowed character is
 263→    // at the X character on the right           X
 264→    class Foo0()
 265→    class Foo6(a: Any, b: Any, c: Any) : FooBar(a, c)
 266→    ```
 267→
 268→Rule id: `class-signature` (`standard` rule set)
 269→
 270→## Discouraged comment location
 271→
 272→Detect discouraged comment locations (no autocorrect).
 273→
 274→!!! note
 275→    Kotlin allows comments to be placed almost everywhere. As this can lead to code which is hard to read, most of them will never be used in practice. Ideally each rule takes comments at all possible locations into account. Sometimes this is really hard and not worth the effort. By explicitly forbidding such comment locations, the development of those rules becomes a bit easier. 
 276→
 277→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
 278→
 279→    ```kotlin
 280→    fun <T> /* some comment */ foo(t: T) = "some-result"
 281→
 282→    fun foo() {
 283→        if (true)
 284→            // some comment
 285→            bar()
 286→    }
 287→    ```
 288→
 289→Rule id: `discouraged-comment-location` (`standard` rule set)
 290→
 291→## Disallow empty lines at start of class body
 292→
 293→Detect blank lines at start of a class body.
 294→
 295→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
 296→
 297→    ```kotlin
 298→    class Foo {
 299→        val foo = "foo"
 300→    }
 301→    ```
 302→
 303→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
 304→
 305→    ```kotlin
 306→    class Foo {
 307→
 308→        val foo = "foo"
 309→    }
 310→    ```
 311→
 312→Rule id: `no-empty-first-line-in-class-body` (`standard` rule set)
 313→
 314→!!! Note
 315→    This rule is only run when `ktlint_code_style` is set to `ktlint_official` or when the rule is enabled explicitly.
 316→
 317→## Disallow consecutive comments
 318→
 319→Consecutive comments are disallowed in following cases:
 320→- Any mix of a consecutive kdoc, a block comment or an EOL comment unless separated by a blank line in between
 321→- Consecutive KDocs (even when separated by a blank line)
 322→- Consecutive block comments (even when separated by a blank line)
 323→
 324→Consecutive EOL comments are always allowed as they are often used instead of a block comment.
 325→
 326→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
 327→
 328→    ```kotlin
 329→    // An EOL comment
 330→    // may be followed by another EOL comment
 331→    val foo = "foo"
 332→
 333→    // Different comment types (including KDoc) may be consecutive ..
 334→
 335→    /*
 336→     * ... but do need to be separated by a blank line ...
 337→     */
 338→
 339→    /**
 340→      * ... but a KDoc can not be followed by an EOL or a block comment or another KDoc
 341→      */
 342→    fun bar() = "bar"
 343→    ```
 344→
 345→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
 346→
 347→    ```kotlin
 348→    /*
 349→     * Block comments can not be consecutive ...
 350→     */
 351→    /*
 352→     * ... even not when separated by a new line.
 353→     */
 354→    val bar = "bar" 
 355→
 356→    /**
 357→      * A KDoc can not be followed by a block comment or an EOL comment or another KDOC
 358→      */
 359→
 360→    // ... even not when separated by a new line.
 361→    ```
 362→
 363→Rule id: `no-consecutive-comments` (`standard` rule set)
 364→
 365→!!! Note
 366→    This rule is only run when `ktlint_code_style` is set to `ktlint_official` or when the rule is enabled explicitly.
 367→
 368→## Function literal
 369→
 370→Enforces the parameters of a function literal and the arrow to be written on the same line as the opening brace if the maximum line length is not exceeded. In case the parameters are wrapped to multiple lines then this is respected.
 371→
 372→If the function literal contains multiple parameter and at least one parameter other than the first parameter starts on a new line than all parameters and the arrow are wrapped to separate lines.
 373→
 374→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
 375→
 376→    ```kotlin
 377→            val foobar1 = { foo + bar }
 378→            val foobar2 =
 379→                {
 380→                    foo + bar
 381→                }
 382→            val foobar3 =
 383→                { foo: Foo ->
 384→                    foo.repeat(2)
 385→                }
 386→            val foobar4 =
 387→                { foo: Foo, bar: Bar ->
 388→                    foo + bar
 389→                }
 390→            val foobar5 = { foo: Foo, bar: Bar -> foo + bar }
 391→            val foobar6 =
 392→                {
 393→                        foo: Foo,
 394→                        bar: Bar
 395→                    ->
 396→                    foo + bar
 397→                }
 398→
 399→    // Assume that the last allowed character is
 400→    // at the X character on the right           X
 401→    val foobar7 =
 402→        barrrrrrrrrrrrrr {
 403→                fooooooooooooooo: Foo
 404→            ->
 405→            foo.repeat(2)
 406→        }
 407→    ```
 408→
 409→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
 410→
 411→    ```kotlin
 412→    val foobar3 =
 413→        {
 414→            foo: Foo ->
 415→            foo.repeat(2)
 416→        }
 417→    val foobar6 =
 418→        { foo: Foo,
 419→          bar: Bar ->
 420→            foo + bar
 421→        }
 422→    // Assume that the last allowed character is
 423→    // at the X character on the right           X
 424→    val foobar7 =
 425→        barrrrrrrrrrrrrr { fooooooooooooooo: Foo ->
 426→            foo.repeat(2)
 427→        }
 428→    ```
 429→
 430→Rule id: `function-literal` (`standard` rule set)
 431→
 432→!!! Note
 433→This rule is only run when `ktlint_code_style` is set to `ktlint_official` or when the rule is enabled explicitly.
 434→
 435→## Function signature
 436→
 437→Rewrites the function signature to a single line when possible (e.g. when not exceeding the `max_line_length` property) or a multiline signature otherwise. In case of function with a body expression, the body expression is placed on the same line as the function signature when not exceeding the `max_line_length` property. Optionally the function signature can be forced to be written as a multiline signature in case the function has more than a specified number of parameters (`.editorconfig` property `ktlint_function_signature_wrapping_rule_always_with_minimum_parameters`)
 438→
 439→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
 440→
 441→    ```kotlin
 442→    // Assume that the last allowed character is
 443→    // at the X character on the right           X
 444→    fun foooooooo(
 445→        a: Any,
 446→        b: Any,
 447→        c: Any
 448→    ): String {
 449→        // body
 450→    }
 451→
 452→    // Assume that the last allowed character is
 453→    // at the X character on the right           X
 454→    fun bar(a: Any, b: Any, c: Any): String {
 455→        // body
 456→    }
 457→
 458→    // When wrapping of body is set to 'default'.
 459→    // Assume that the last allowed character is
 460→    // at the X character on the right           X
 461→    fun f(a: Any, b: Any): String = "some-result"
 462→        .uppercase()
 463→
 464→    // When wrapping of body is set to 'multiline'
 465→    // or 'always'.
 466→    // Assume that the last allowed character is
 467→    // at the X character on the right           X
 468→    fun f(a: Any, b: Any): String =
 469→        "some-result"
 470→            .uppercase()
 471→    ```
 472→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
 473→
 474→    ```kotlin
 475→    // Assume that the last allowed character is
 476→    // at the X character on the right           X
 477→    fun foooooooo(a: Any, b: Any, c: Any): String {
 478→        // body
 479→    }
 480→
 481→    // Assume that the last allowed character is
 482→    // at the X character on the right           X
 483→    fun bar(
 484→        a: Any,
 485→        b: Any,
 486→        c: Any
 487→    ): String {
 488→        // body
 489→    }
 490→
 491→    // When wrapping of body is set to 'default'.
 492→    // Assume that the last allowed character is
 493→    // at the X character on the right           X
 494→    fun f(a: Any, b: Any): String =
 495→        "some-result"
 496→            .uppercase()
 497→
 498→    // When wrapping of body is set to 'multiline'
 499→    // or 'always'.
 500→    // Assume that the last allowed character is
 501→    // at the X character on the right           X
 502→    fun f(a: Any, b: Any): String = "some-result"
 503→        .uppercase()
 504→    ```
 505→
 506→Rule id: `function-signature` (`standard` rule set)
 507→
 508→## If else bracing
 509→
 510→If at least one branch of an if-else statement or an if-else-if statement is wrapped between curly braces then all branches should be wrapped between braces.
 511→
 512→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
 513→
 514→    ```kotlin
 515→    fun foo(value: int) {
 516→        if (value > 0) {
 517→            doSomething()
 518→        } else if (value < 0) {
 519→            doSomethingElse()
 520→        } else {
 521→            doSomethingElse2()
 522→        }
 523→    }
 524→    ```
 525→
 526→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
 527→
 528→    ```kotlin
 529→    fun foo(value: int) {
 530→        if (value > 0)
 531→            doSomething()
 532→        else if (value < 0) {
 533→            doSomethingElse()
 534→        } else
 535→            doSomethingElse2()
 536→    }
 537→    ```
 538→
 539→Rule id: `if-else-bracing` (`standard` rule set)
 540→
 541→!!! Note
 542→    This rule is only run when `ktlint_code_style` is set to `ktlint_official` or when the rule is enabled explicitly.
 543→
 544→## If else wrapping
 545→
 546→A single line if-statement should be kept simple. It may contain no more than one else-branch. The branches may not be wrapped in a block.
 547→
 548→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
 549→
 550→    ```kotlin
 551→    fun foobar() {
 552→        if (true) foo()
 553→        if (true) foo() else bar()
 554→    }
 555→    ```
 556→
 557→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
 558→
 559→    ```kotlin
 560→    fun foobar() {
 561→        if (true) if (false) foo() else bar()
 562→        if (true) bar() else if (false) foo() else bar()
 563→        if (true) { foo() } else bar()
 564→        if (true) bar() else { if (false) foo() else bar() }
 565→    }
 566→    ```
 567→
 568→Rule id: `if-else-wrapping` (`standard` rule set)
 569→
 570→!!! Note
 571→    This rule is only run when `ktlint_code_style` is set to `ktlint_official` or when the rule is enabled explicitly.
 572→
 573→## Naming
 574→
 575→### Function naming
 576→
 577→Enforce naming of function. 
 578→
 579→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
 580→
 581→    ```kotlin
 582→    fun foo() {}
 583→    fun fooBar() {}
 584→    ```
 585→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint Test"
 586→
 587→    ```kotlin
 588→    @Test
 589→    fun `Some name`() {}
 590→
 591→    @Test
 592→    fun do_something() {}
 593→    ```
 594→
 595→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
 596→
 597→    ```kotlin
 598→    fun Foo() {}
 599→    fun Foo_Bar() {}
 600→    fun `Some name`() {}
 601→    fun do_something() {}
 602→    ```
 603→
 604→!!! note
 605→    Functions in files which import a class from package `org.junit`, `org.testng` or `kotlin.test` are considered to be test functions. Functions in such classes are allowed to have underscores in the name. Or function names can be specified between backticks and do not need to adhere to the normal naming convention.
 606→
 607→This rule can also be suppressed with the IntelliJ IDEA inspection suppression `FunctionName`.
 608→
 609→Rule id: `function-naming` (`standard` rule set)
 610→
 611→### Package naming
 612→
 613→Enforce naming of package.
 614→
 615→This rule can also be suppressed with the IntelliJ IDEA inspection suppression `PackageName`.
 616→
 617→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
 618→
 619→    ```kotlin
 620→    package foo
 621→    package foo.foo
 622→    package foo_bar
 623→    package foo.foo_bar
 624→    ```
 625→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
 626→
 627→    ```kotlin
 628→    package Foo
 629→    package foo.Foo
 630→    package `foo bar`
 631→    package foo.`foo bar`
 632→    ```
 633→
 634→Rule id: `package-naming` (`standard` rule set)
 635→
 636→### Property naming
 637→
 638→Enforce naming of property.
 639→
 640→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
 641→
 642→    ```kotlin
 643→    val FOO_1 = Foo()
 644→    val FOO_BAR_1 = "FOO-BAR"
 645→
 646→    var foo1: Foo = Foo()
 647→
 648→    class Bar {
 649→        const val FOO_2 = "foo"
 650→        const val FOO_BAR_2 = "FOO-BAR"
 651→
 652→        val foo2 = "foo"
 653→        val fooBar2 = "foo-bar"
 654→
 655→        // Backing property
 656→        private val _elementList = mutableListOf<Element>()
 657→        val elementList: List<Element>
 658→            get() = _elementList
 659→    }
 660→    ```
 661→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
 662→
 663→    ```kotlin
 664→    val Foo1 = Foo()
 665→    val FooBar1 = "FOO-BAR"
 666→
 667→    var FOO_1: Foo = Foo()
 668→
 669→    class Bar {
 670→        const val foo2 = "foo"
 671→        const val fooBar2 = "FOO-BAR"
 672→
 673→        val FOO2 = "foo"
 674→        val FOO_BAR_2 = "foo-bar"
 675→
 676→        // Incomplete backing property as public property 'elementList1' is missing
 677→        private val _elementList1 = mutableListOf<Element>()
 678→
 679→        // Invalid backing property as '_elementList2' is not a private property
 680→        val _elementList2 = mutableListOf<Element>()
 681→        val elementList2: List<Element>
 682→            get() = _elementList2
 683→    }
 684→    ```
 685→
 686→!!! note
 687→    Top level `val` properties and `const val` properties have to be written in screaming snake notation. Local `val` and `const val` are written in lower camel case.
 688→
 689→This rule can also be suppressed with the IntelliJ IDEA inspection suppression `PropertyName`.
 690→
 691→Rule id: `property-naming` (`standard` rule set)
 692→
 693→## No empty file
 694→
 695→A kotlin (script) file should not be empty. It needs to contain at least one declaration. Files only contain a package and/or import statements are as of that disallowed.
 696→
 697→Rule id: `no-empty-file`
 698→
 699→## No single line block comments
 700→
 701→A single line block comment should be replaced with an EOL comment when possible.
 702→
 703→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
 704→
 705→    ```kotlin
 706→    /*
 707→     * Some comment
 708→     */
 709→    val foo = "foo" // Some comment
 710→    val foo = { /* no-op */ }
 711→    ```
 712→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
 713→
 714→    ```kotlin
 715→    /* Some comment */
 716→    val foo = "foo" /* Some comment */
 717→    ```
 718→
 719→Rule id: `no-single-line-block-comment` (`standard` rule set)

 721→## Spacing
 722→
 723→### No blank lines in list

 737→    ```

 744→        Foo,
 745→
 746→        Bar
 747→
 748→    {

 881→### Parameter list spacing
 882→
 883→Consistent spacing inside the parameter list.
 884→
 885→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
 886→
 887→    ```kotlin
 888→    fun foo(a: Any ) = "some-result"
 889→    fun foo() = "some-result"
 890→    ```
 891→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
 892→
 893→    ```kotlin
 894→    fun foo( a : Any ) = "some-result"
 895→    fun foo(
 896→    ) = "some-result"
 897→    ```
 898→
 899→Rule id: `parameter-list-spacing` (`standard` rule set)
 900→
 901→### String template indent
 902→
 903→Enforce consistent string template indentation for multiline string templates which are post-fixed with `.trimIndent()`. The opening and closing `"""` are placed on separate lines and the indentation of the content of the template is aligned with the `"""`.
 904→
 905→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
 906→
 907→    ```kotlin
 908→    val foo =
 909→        """
 910→        line1
 911→        line2
 912→        """.trimIndent()
 913→    fun foo() {
 914→        // The opening """ can not be wrapped to next line as that would result in a compilation error
 915→        return """
 916→            line1
 917→            line2
 918→            """.trimIndent()
 919→    }
 920→    ```
 921→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
 922→
 923→    ```kotlin
 924→    val foo = """
 925→              line1
 926→              line2
 927→              """.trimIndent()
 928→    fun foo() {
 929→        return """
 930→            line1
 931→            line2
 932→        """.trimIndent()
 933→    }
 934→    ```
 935→
 936→Rule id: `string-template-indent` (`standard` rule set)
 937→
 938→!!! Note
 939→    This rule is only run when `ktlint_code_style` is set to `ktlint_official` or when the rule is enabled explicitly.
 940→
 941→### Try catch finally spacing
 942→
 943→Enforce consistent spacing in `try { .. } catch { .. } finally { .. }`.
 944→
 945→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
 946→
 947→    ```kotlin
 948→    fun foo() =
 949→        try {
 950→            // do something
 951→        } catch (exception: Exception) {
 952→            // handle exception
 953→        } finally {
 954→            // clean up
 955→        }
 956→    ```
 957→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
 958→
 959→    ```kotlin
 960→    fun foo1() = try { /* ... */ } catch (exception: Exception) { /* ... */ } finally { /* ... */ }
 961→    fun foo2() = 
 962→        try {
 963→            // do something
 964→        }
 965→        catch (exception: Exception) {
 966→            // handle exception
 967→        }
 968→        finally {
 969→            // clean up
 970→        }
 971→    ```
 972→
 973→Rule id: `try-catch-finally-spacing` (`standard` rule set)
 974→
 975→!!! Note
 976→    This rule is only run when `ktlint_code_style` is set to `ktlint_official` or when the rule is enabled explicitly.
 977→

 996→    }

 999→Rule id: `type-argument-list-spacing` (`standard` rule set)
1000→
1001→### Type parameter list spacing
1002→
1003→Spacing after a type parameter list in function and class declarations.
1004→
1005→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
1006→
1007→    ```kotlin
1008→    fun <T> foo1(t: T) = "some-result"
1009→    fun <T> foo2(t: T) = "some-result"
1010→    fun <T> foo3(t: T) = "some-result"
1011→    ```
1012→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
1013→
1014→    ```kotlin
1015→    fun<T> foo1(t: T) = "some-result"
1016→    fun <T>foo2(t: T) = "some-result"
1017→    fun<T>foo3(t: T) = "some-result"

1020→Rule id: `type-parameter-list-spacing` (`standard` rule set)
1021→
1022→## Wrapping
1023→
1024→### Content receiver wrapping
1025→
1026→Wraps the content receiver list to a separate line regardless of maximum line length. If the maximum line length is configured and is exceeded, wrap the context receivers and if needed its projection types to separate lines.
1027→
1028→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
1029→
1030→    ```kotlin
1031→    // ALways wrap regardless of whether max line length is set
1032→    context(Foo)
1033→    fun fooBar()
1034→
1035→    // Wrap each context receiver to a separate line when the
1036→    // entire context receiver list does not fit on a single line
1037→    context(
1038→        Fooooooooooooooooooo1,
1039→        Foooooooooooooooooooooooooooooo2
1040→    )
1041→    fun fooBar()
1042→
1043→    // Wrap each context receiver to a separate line when the
1044→    // entire context receiver list does not fit on a single line.
1045→    // Also, wrap each of it projection types in case a context
1046→    // receiver does not fit on a single line after it has been
1047→    // wrapped.
1048→    context(
1049→        Foooooooooooooooo<
1050→            Foo,
1051→            Bar
1052→            >
1053→    )
1054→    fun fooBar()
1055→    ```
1056→
1057→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
1058→
1059→    ```kotlin
1060→    // Should be wrapped regardless of whether max line length is set
1061→    context(Foo) fun fooBar()
1062→
1063→    // Should be wrapped when the entire context receiver list does not
1064→    // fit on a single line
1065→    context(Fooooooooooooooooooo1, Foooooooooooooooooooooooooooooo2)
1066→    fun fooBar()
1067→
1068→    // Should be wrapped when the entire context receiver list does not
1069→    // fit on a single line. Also, it should wrap each of it projection
1070→    // type in case a context receiver does not fit on a single line 
1071→    // after it has been wrapped.
1072→    context(Foooooooooooooooo<Foo, Bar>)
1073→    fun fooBar()
1074→    ```
1075→
1076→Rule id: `context-receiver-wrapping` (`standard` rule set)
1077→
1078→### Enum wrapping
1079→
1080→An enum should be a single line, or each enum entry has to be placed on a separate line. In case the enumeration contains enum entries and declarations those are to be separated by a blank line.
1081→
1082→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
1083→
1084→    ```kotlin
1085→    enum class Foo { A, B, C, D }
1086→
1087→    enum class Foo {
1088→        A,
1089→        B,
1090→        C,
1091→        D,
1092→        ;
1093→
1094→        fun foo() = "foo"
1095→    }
1096→    ```
1097→
1098→=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Disallowed"
1099→
1100→    ```kotlin
1101→    enum class Foo {
1102→        A,
1103→        B, C,
1104→        D
1105→    }
1106→
1107→    enum class Foo {
1108→        A;
1109→        fun foo() = "foo"
1110→    }
1111→    ```
1112→
1113→Rule id: `enum-wrapping` (`standard` rule set)
1114→
1115→### Multiline expression wrapping
1116→
1117→Multiline expression on the right hand side of an expression are forced to start on a separate line. Expressions in return statement are excluded as that would result in a compilation error. 
1118→
1119→=== "[:material-heart:](#) Ktlint"
1120→
1121→    ```kotlin

content.thinking:
Now I understand the issue. Let me look at the `PropertyNamingRule.kt` more closely.

The issue is in the `isObjectValue()` function. It checks if a property is an object value and if so, it requires SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE. But according to the Android style guide, mutable collections should not use SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE.

Looking at the code:
1. `isObjectValue()` checks if the property is in an OBJECT_DECLARATION and is a val (not var)
2. `visitConstProperty()` is called for properties that have const modifier, are top level values, or object values

The problem is that mutable collections (like `MutableMap`, `MutableList`, etc.) are not constants even when declared as `val` because their contents can be modified. According to the Android style guide, these should use lower camel case, not SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE.

Let me look at the existing tests for this rule to understand how it's being tested.

Now I understand the issue and the codebase. The problem is that in `PropertyNamingRule.kt`, the `isObjectValue()` function considers all `val` properties in objects as requiring SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE. However, according to the Android Kotlin style guide, mutable collections (like `MutableMap`, `MutableList`, `MutableSet`) should NOT use SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE even when declared as `val` because their contents can be mutated.

Looking at the code, I need to modify the logic to check if the property type is a mutable collection type. If it is, then the property should use lower camel case, not SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE.

I need to:
1. Add a function to check if the property type is a mutable collection type
2. Modify the condition in `visitProperty` to exclude mutable collections from the const-like naming requirement

content.thinking:
Now I need to understand the AST structure for a property with a type reference. Looking at the code, I can see that `TYPE_REFERENCE` is child of PROPERTY, and it contains `USER_TYPE` which contains the actual type name.

For a property like:
```kotlin
val someMap: MutableMap<Key, Value> = mutableMapOf()
```

The AST structure would be something like:
- PROPERTY
  - VAL_KEYWORD
  - IDENTIFIER (someMap)
  - COLON
  - TYPE_REFERENCE
    - USER_TYPE
      - IDENTIFIER (MutableMap)
      - TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST
        - ...
  - EQ
  - CALL_EXPRESSION
    - ...

So to detect mutable collection types, I need to:
1. Find the TYPE_REFERENCE child of the property
2. Get the type name (e.g., MutableMap, MutableList, MutableSet, etc.)
3. Check if it's one of the known mutable collection types

The mutable collection types according to Kotlin/Android style guide that should NOT use SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE:
- MutableList
- MutableSet
- MutableMap
- MutableCollection
- MutableIterable
- MutableIterator
- ArrayList
- LinkedList
- HashSet
- LinkedHashSet
- TreeSet
- HashMap
- LinkedHashMap
- TreeMap
- etc.

Actually, looking at the Android style guide more carefully:
https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide#constant_names

Constants are `val` properties with no custom `get` function, whose contents are deeply immutable, and whose functions have no detectable side-effects. This includes immutable types and immutable collections of immutable types as well as scalars and string if marked as const.

So the key distinction is:
- Immutable types (like `List`, `Set`, `Map`, etc.) with immutable contents should use SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE


- Mutable types (like `MutableList`, `MutableSet`, `MutableMap`, etc.) should NOT use SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE because their contents can be modified

I'll focus on creating a comprehensive list of mutable collection types to check against. These types inherently allow modification, so they don't qualify for constant naming conventions. I'll need to build a robust check that captures all variations of mutable collection types, ensuring accurate identification of properties that should use standard camelCase naming.

The problem is that in `PropertyNamingRule.kt`, properties of mutable collection types (like `MutableMap`, `MutableList`, etc.) are being treated as constants requiring SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE, but according to the Android Kotlin style guide, mutable collections are NOT constants because their contents can be changed.

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.CLASS_BODY
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.CONST_KEYWORD
  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.FILE
  6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.GET_KEYWORD
  7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.IDENTIFIER
  8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.MODIFIER_LIST
  9→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.OBJECT_DECLARATION
 10→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.OVERRIDE_KEYWORD
 11→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.PRIVATE_KEYWORD
 12→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.PROPERTY
 13→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.PROPERTY_ACCESSOR
 14→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.VAL_KEYWORD
 15→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule
 16→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
 17→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.children
 18→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
 19→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules.internal.regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters
 20→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 21→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.tree.IElementType
 22→
 23→/**
 24→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#function-names
 25→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#property-names
 26→ */
 27→public class PropertyNamingRule :
 28→    StandardRule("property-naming"),
 29→    Rule.Experimental {
 30→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 31→        node: ASTNode,
 32→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 33→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 34→    ) {
 35→        node
 36→            .takeIf { node.elementType == PROPERTY }
 37→            ?.let { property -> visitProperty(property, emit) }
 38→    }
 39→
 40→    private fun visitProperty(
 41→        property: ASTNode,
 42→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 43→    ) {
 44→        property
 45→            .findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
 46→            ?.let { identifier ->
 47→                when {
 48→                    property.hasCustomGetter() -> {
 49→                        // Can not reliably determine whether the value is immutable or not
 50→                    }
 51→                    property.isBackingProperty() -> {
 52→                        visitBackingProperty(identifier, emit)
 53→                    }
 54→                    property.hasConstModifier() ||
 55→                        property.isTopLevelValue() ||
 56→                        property.isObjectValue() -> {
 57→                        visitConstProperty(identifier, emit)
 58→                    }
 59→                    else -> {
 60→                        visitNonConstProperty(identifier, emit)
 61→                    }
 62→                }
 63→            }
 64→    }
 65→
 66→    private fun visitBackingProperty(
 67→        identifier: ASTNode,
 68→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 69→    ) {
 70→        identifier
 71→            .text
 72→            .takeUnless { it.matches(BACKING_PROPERTY_LOWER_CAMEL_CASE_REGEXP) }
 73→            ?.let {
 74→                emit(identifier.startOffset, "Backing property name should start with underscore followed by lower camel case", false)
 75→            }
 76→    }
 77→
 78→    private fun visitConstProperty(
 79→        identifier: ASTNode,
 80→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 81→    ) {
 82→        identifier
 83→            .text
 84→            .takeUnless {
 85→                // Allow
 86→                // object Foo {
 87→                //     private const val serialVersionUID: Long = 123
 88→                // }
 89→                it == SERIAL_VERSION_UID_PROPERTY_NAME
 90→            }?.takeUnless { it.matches(SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE_REGEXP) }
 91→            ?.let {
 92→                emit(
 93→                    identifier.startOffset,
 94→                    "Property name should use the screaming snake case notation when the value can not be changed",
 95→                    false,
 96→                )
 97→            }
 98→    }
 99→
100→    private fun visitNonConstProperty(
101→        identifier: ASTNode,
102→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
103→    ) {
104→        identifier
105→            .text
106→            .takeUnless { it.matches(LOWER_CAMEL_CASE_REGEXP) }
107→            ?.let {
108→                emit(identifier.startOffset, "Property name should start with a lowercase letter and use camel case", false)
109→            }
110→    }
111→
112→    private fun ASTNode.hasCustomGetter() = findChildByType(PROPERTY_ACCESSOR)?.findChildByType(GET_KEYWORD) != null
113→
114→    private fun ASTNode.hasConstModifier() = hasModifier(CONST_KEYWORD)
115→
116→    private fun ASTNode.hasModifier(iElementType: IElementType) =
117→        findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)
118→            ?.children()
119→            .orEmpty()
120→            .any { it.elementType == iElementType }
121→
122→    private fun ASTNode.isTopLevelValue() = treeParent.elementType == FILE && containsValKeyword()
123→
124→    private fun ASTNode.containsValKeyword() = children().any { it.elementType == VAL_KEYWORD }
125→
126→    private fun ASTNode.isObjectValue() =
127→        treeParent.elementType == CLASS_BODY &&
128→            treeParent?.treeParent?.elementType == OBJECT_DECLARATION &&
129→            containsValKeyword() &&
130→            !hasModifier(OVERRIDE_KEYWORD)
131→
132→    private fun ASTNode.isBackingProperty() =
133→        takeIf { hasModifier(PRIVATE_KEYWORD) }
134→            ?.findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
135→            ?.takeIf { it.text.startsWith("_") }
136→            ?.let { identifier ->
137→                this.hasPublicProperty(identifier.text.removePrefix("_"))
138→            }
139→            ?: false
140→
141→    private fun ASTNode.hasPublicProperty(identifier: String) =
142→        treeParent
143→            .children()
144→            .filter { it.elementType == PROPERTY }
145→            .mapNotNull { it.findChildByType(IDENTIFIER) }
146→            .any { it.text == identifier }
147→
148→    private companion object {
149→        val LOWER_CAMEL_CASE_REGEXP = "[a-z][a-zA-Z0-9]*".regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters()
150→        val SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE_REGEXP = "[A-Z][_A-Z0-9]*".regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters()
151→        val BACKING_PROPERTY_LOWER_CAMEL_CASE_REGEXP = "_[a-z][a-zA-Z0-9]*".regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters()
152→        const val SERIAL_VERSION_UID_PROPERTY_NAME = "serialVersionUID"
153→    }
154→}
155→
156→public val PROPERTY_NAMING_RULE_ID: RuleId = PropertyNamingRule().ruleId

        treeParent.elementType == CLASS_BODY &&

    /**
     * Checks if the property has a mutable type (e.g., MutableList, MutableMap, etc.).
     * Mutable properties are not considered constants even when declared as `val` at top-level or in objects,
     * because their contents can be modified.
     * See: https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide#constant_names
     */
    private fun ASTNode.isMutableProperty(): Boolean {
        val typeReference = findChildByType(TYPE_REFERENCE) ?: return false
        val typeName = typeReference.findTypeNameWithoutPackage()
        return typeName != null && typeName in MUTABLE_TYPES
    }

    private fun ASTNode.findTypeNameWithoutPackage(): String? {
        // Handle nullable types by unwrapping them first
        val userType =
            findChildByType(USER_TYPE)
                ?: findChildByType(NULLABLE_TYPE)?.findChildByType(USER_TYPE)
                ?: return null
        // Get the first identifier which is the type name (without package prefix)
        return userType.findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)?.text
    }

    private fun ASTNode.isBackingProperty() ="}

        /**

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules

  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.CLASS_BODY

 22→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules.internal.regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters
 23→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 24→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.tree.IElementType
 25→
 26→/**
 27→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#function-names
 28→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#property-names
 29→ */
 30→public class PropertyNamingRule :
 31→    StandardRule("property-naming"),
 32→    Rule.Experimental {
 33→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 34→        node: ASTNode,
 35→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 36→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 37→    ) {
 38→        node
 39→            .takeIf { node.elementType == PROPERTY }
 40→            ?.let { property -> visitProperty(property, emit) }
 41→    }
 42→
 43→    private fun visitProperty(
 44→        property: ASTNode,
 45→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 46→    ) {
 47→        property
 48→            .findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
 49→            ?.let { identifier ->
 50→                when {
 51→                    property.hasCustomGetter() -> {
 52→                        // Can not reliably determine whether the value is immutable or not
 53→                    }
 54→                    property.isBackingProperty() -> {
 55→                        visitBackingProperty(identifier, emit)
 56→                    }
 57→                    property.hasConstModifier() -> {
 58→                        visitConstProperty(identifier, emit)
 59→                    }
 60→                    (property.isTopLevelValue() || property.isObjectValue()) &&
 61→                        !property.isMutableProperty() -> {
 62→                        visitConstProperty(identifier, emit)
 63→                    }
 64→                    else -> {
 65→                        visitNonConstProperty(identifier, emit)
 66→                    }
 67→                }
 68→            }
 69→    }
 70→
 71→    private fun visitBackingProperty(
 72→        identifier: ASTNode,
 73→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 74→    ) {
 75→        identifier
 76→            .text
 77→            .takeUnless { it.matches(BACKING_PROPERTY_LOWER_CAMEL_CASE_REGEXP) }
 78→            ?.let {
 79→                emit(identifier.startOffset, "Backing property name should start with underscore followed by lower camel case", false)
 80→            }
 81→    }
 82→
 83→    private fun visitConstProperty(
 84→        identifier: ASTNode,
 85→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 86→    ) {
 87→        identifier
 88→            .text
 89→            .takeUnless {
 90→                // Allow
 91→                // object Foo {
 92→                //     private const val serialVersionUID: Long = 123
 93→                // }
 94→                it == SERIAL_VERSION_UID_PROPERTY_NAME
 95→            }?.takeUnless { it.matches(SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE_REGEXP) }
 96→            ?.let {
 97→                emit(
 98→                    identifier.startOffset,
 99→                    "Property name should use the screaming snake case notation when the value can not be changed",
100→                    false,
101→                )
102→            }
103→    }
104→
105→    private fun visitNonConstProperty(
106→        identifier: ASTNode,
107→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
108→    ) {
109→        identifier
110→            .text
111→            .takeUnless { it.matches(LOWER_CAMEL_CASE_REGEXP) }
112→            ?.let {
113→                emit(identifier.startOffset, "Property name should start with a lowercase letter and use camel case", false)
114→            }
115→    }
116→
117→    private fun ASTNode.hasCustomGetter() = findChildByType(PROPERTY_ACCESSOR)?.findChildByType(GET_KEYWORD) != null
118→
119→    private fun ASTNode.hasConstModifier() = hasModifier(CONST_KEYWORD)
120→
121→    private fun ASTNode.hasModifier(iElementType: IElementType) =
122→        findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)
123→            ?.children()
124→            .orEmpty()
125→            .any { it.elementType == iElementType }
126→
127→    private fun ASTNode.isTopLevelValue() = treeParent.elementType == FILE && containsValKeyword()
128→
129→    private fun ASTNode.containsValKeyword() = children().any { it.elementType == VAL_KEYWORD }
130→
131→    private fun ASTNode.isObjectValue() =
132→        treeParent.elementType == CLASS_BODY &&
133→            treeParent?.treeParent?.elementType == OBJECT_DECLARATION &&
134→            containsValKeyword() &&
135→            !hasModifier(OVERRIDE_KEYWORD)
136→
137→    /**
138→     * Checks if the property has a mutable type (e.g., MutableList, MutableMap, etc.).
139→     * Mutable properties are not considered constants even when declared as `val` at top-level or in objects,
140→     * because their contents can be modified.
141→     * See: https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide#constant_names
142→     */
143→    private fun ASTNode.isMutableProperty(): Boolean {
144→        val typeReference = findChildByType(TYPE_REFERENCE) ?: return false
145→        val typeName = typeReference.findTypeNameWithoutPackage()
146→        return typeName != null && typeName in MUTABLE_TYPES
147→    }
148→
149→    private fun ASTNode.findTypeNameWithoutPackage(): String? {
150→        // Handle nullable types by unwrapping them first
151→        val userType =
152→            findChildByType(USER_TYPE)
153→                ?: findChildByType(NULLABLE_TYPE)?.findChildByType(USER_TYPE)
154→                ?: return null
155→        // Get the first identifier which is the type name (without package prefix)
156→        return userType.findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)?.text
157→    }
158→
159→    private fun ASTNode.isBackingProperty() =
160→        takeIf { hasModifier(PRIVATE_KEYWORD) }
161→            ?.findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
162→            ?.takeIf { it.text.startsWith("_") }
163→            ?.let { identifier ->
164→                this.hasPublicProperty(identifier.text.removePrefix("_"))
165→            }
166→            ?: false
167→
168→    private fun ASTNode.hasPublicProperty(identifier: String) =
169→        treeParent
170→            .children()
171→            .filter { it.elementType == PROPERTY }
172→            .mapNotNull { it.findChildByType(IDENTIFIER) }
173→            .any { it.text == identifier }
174→
175→    private companion object {
176→        val LOWER_CAMEL_CASE_REGEXP = "[a-z][a-zA-Z0-9]*".regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters()
177→        val SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE_REGEXP = "[A-Z][_A-Z0-9]*".regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters()
178→        val BACKING_PROPERTY_LOWER_CAMEL_CASE_REGEXP = "_[a-z][a-zA-Z0-9]*".regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters()
179→        const val SERIAL_VERSION_UID_PROPERTY_NAME = "serialVersionUID"
180→
181→        /**
182→         * Mutable types are not considered constant even when declared as `val` because their contents can be modified.
183→         * See: https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide#constant_names
184→         */
185→        val MUTABLE_TYPES =
186→            setOf(
187→                // Kotlin mutable collections
188→                "MutableCollection",
189→                "MutableList",
190→                "MutableSet",
191→                "MutableMap",
192→                "MutableIterator",
193→                "MutableListIterator",
194→                // Common mutable types from Java
195→                "ArrayList",
196→                "LinkedList",
197→                "HashSet",
198→                "LinkedHashSet",
199→                "TreeSet",
200→                "HashMap",
201→                "LinkedHashMap",
202→                "TreeMap",
203→                "Hashtable",
204→                "ArrayDeque",
205→                // Other mutable types
206→                "StringBuilder",
207→                "StringBuffer",
208→            )
209→    }
210→}
211→

content.thinking:
The tests are failing. This means that the implementation is not correctly detecting the mutable types. Let me debug this by looking at the AST structure more closely. The issue might be that `findChildByType(TYPE_REFERENCE)` is being called on the PROPERTY node, but I need to make sure the type reference is correctly detected.

Let me look at a simpler test case and see what's happening. Let me trace through:

For a property like:
```kotlin
object Foo {
    val someMap: MutableMap<String, String> = mutableMapOf()
}
```

The AST would be:
- PROPERTY
  - VAL_KEYWORD
  - IDENTIFIER (someMap)
  - COLON
  - TYPE_REFERENCE
    - USER_TYPE
      - IDENTIFIER (MutableMap)
      - TYPE_ARGUMENT_LIST
        - ...
  - EQ
  - CALL_EXPRESSION
    - ...

So the call `findChildByType(TYPE_REFERENCE)` on the PROPERTY should work. Let me trace through my implementation:

1. `property.isMutableProperty()`:
   - `findChildByType(TYPE_REFERENCE)` - should return the TYPE_REFERENCE node
   - `typeReference.findTypeNameWithoutPackage()` - this is called on the TYPE_REFERENCE node
   
In `findTypeNameWithoutPackage()`:
   - `findChildByType(USER_TYPE)` - this is looking for USER_TYPE as a direct child of TYPE_REFERENCE
   - `findChildByType(NULLABLE_TYPE)?.findChildByType(USER_TYPE)` - for nullable types

This looks correct... Wait, maybe the issue is that I'm checking `isMutableProperty()` but if it returns `false`, the property will not be treated as const or non-const - it will fall through to the else branch which treats it as non-const.

Actually, looking at the condition again:


The condition checks if the property is a top-level or object value and not a mutable property. If `isMutableProperty()` returns `true`, the condition becomes false, triggering the else branch to call `visitNonConstProperty`. 

I suspect the problem lies in how `findTypeNameWithoutPackage()` handles type argument lists. Something might be interfering with the type detection mechanism.

The method searches for the type name by first finding the USER_TYPE node, potentially handling nullable types. It extracts the first identifier as the type name, but there could be complications with complex type structures.

I'll investigate the USER_TYPE parsing more closely, examining how different type representations are structured in the actual code. The current approach might need refinement to handle more intricate type references.

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.CLASS_BODY
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.CONST_KEYWORD
  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.FILE
  6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.GET_KEYWORD
  7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.IDENTIFIER
  8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.MODIFIER_LIST
  9→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.OBJECT_DECLARATION
 10→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.OVERRIDE_KEYWORD
 11→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.PRIVATE_KEYWORD
 12→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.PROPERTY
 13→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.PROPERTY_ACCESSOR
 14→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.TYPE_REFERENCE
 15→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.VAL_KEYWORD
 16→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule
 17→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
 18→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.children
 19→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
 20→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules.internal.regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters
 21→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 22→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.tree.IElementType
 23→
 24→/**
 25→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#function-names
 26→ * https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#property-names
 27→ */
 28→public class PropertyNamingRule :
 29→    StandardRule("property-naming"),
 30→    Rule.Experimental {
 31→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 32→        node: ASTNode,
 33→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 34→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 35→    ) {
 36→        node
 37→            .takeIf { node.elementType == PROPERTY }
 38→            ?.let { property -> visitProperty(property, emit) }
 39→    }
 40→
 41→    private fun visitProperty(
 42→        property: ASTNode,
 43→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 44→    ) {
 45→        property
 46→            .findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
 47→            ?.let { identifier ->
 48→                when {
 49→                    property.hasCustomGetter() -> {
 50→                        // Can not reliably determine whether the value is immutable or not
 51→                    }
 52→                    property.isBackingProperty() -> {
 53→                        visitBackingProperty(identifier, emit)
 54→                    }
 55→                    property.hasConstModifier() -> {
 56→                        visitConstProperty(identifier, emit)
 57→                    }
 58→                    (property.isTopLevelValue() || property.isObjectValue()) &&
 59→                        !property.isMutableProperty() -> {
 60→                        visitConstProperty(identifier, emit)
 61→                    }
 62→                    else -> {
 63→                        visitNonConstProperty(identifier, emit)
 64→                    }
 65→                }
 66→            }
 67→    }
 68→
 69→    private fun visitBackingProperty(
 70→        identifier: ASTNode,
 71→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 72→    ) {
 73→        identifier
 74→            .text
 75→            .takeUnless { it.matches(BACKING_PROPERTY_LOWER_CAMEL_CASE_REGEXP) }
 76→            ?.let {
 77→                emit(identifier.startOffset, "Backing property name should start with underscore followed by lower camel case", false)
 78→            }
 79→    }
 80→
 81→    private fun visitConstProperty(
 82→        identifier: ASTNode,
 83→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 84→    ) {
 85→        identifier
 86→            .text
 87→            .takeUnless {
 88→                // Allow
 89→                // object Foo {
 90→                //     private const val serialVersionUID: Long = 123
 91→                // }
 92→                it == SERIAL_VERSION_UID_PROPERTY_NAME
 93→            }?.takeUnless { it.matches(SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE_REGEXP) }
 94→            ?.let {
 95→                emit(
 96→                    identifier.startOffset,
 97→                    "Property name should use the screaming snake case notation when the value can not be changed",
 98→                    false,
 99→                )
100→            }
101→    }
102→
103→    private fun visitNonConstProperty(
104→        identifier: ASTNode,
105→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
106→    ) {
107→        identifier
108→            .text
109→            .takeUnless { it.matches(LOWER_CAMEL_CASE_REGEXP) }
110→            ?.let {
111→                emit(identifier.startOffset, "Property name should start with a lowercase letter and use camel case", false)
112→            }
113→    }
114→
115→    private fun ASTNode.hasCustomGetter() = findChildByType(PROPERTY_ACCESSOR)?.findChildByType(GET_KEYWORD) != null
116→
117→    private fun ASTNode.hasConstModifier() = hasModifier(CONST_KEYWORD)
118→
119→    private fun ASTNode.hasModifier(iElementType: IElementType) =
120→        findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)
121→            ?.children()
122→            .orEmpty()
123→            .any { it.elementType == iElementType }
124→
125→    private fun ASTNode.isTopLevelValue() = treeParent.elementType == FILE && containsValKeyword()
126→
127→    private fun ASTNode.containsValKeyword() = children().any { it.elementType == VAL_KEYWORD }
128→
129→    private fun ASTNode.isObjectValue() =
130→        treeParent.elementType == CLASS_BODY &&
131→            treeParent?.treeParent?.elementType == OBJECT_DECLARATION &&
132→            containsValKeyword() &&
133→            !hasModifier(OVERRIDE_KEYWORD)
134→
135→    /**
136→     * Checks if the property has a mutable type (e.g., MutableList, MutableMap, etc.).
137→     * Mutable properties are not considered constants even when declared as `val` at top-level or in objects,
138→     * because their contents can be modified.
139→     * See: https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide#constant_names
140→     */
141→    private fun ASTNode.isMutableProperty(): Boolean {
142→        val typeReference = findChildByType(TYPE_REFERENCE) ?: return false
143→        val typeText = typeReference.text.removePrefix("?").trimStart()
144→        return MUTABLE_TYPES.any { typeText.startsWith(it) }
145→    }
146→
147→    private fun ASTNode.isBackingProperty() =
148→        takeIf { hasModifier(PRIVATE_KEYWORD) }
149→            ?.findChildByType(IDENTIFIER)
150→            ?.takeIf { it.text.startsWith("_") }
151→            ?.let { identifier ->
152→                this.hasPublicProperty(identifier.text.removePrefix("_"))
153→            }
154→            ?: false
155→
156→    private fun ASTNode.hasPublicProperty(identifier: String) =
157→        treeParent
158→            .children()
159→            .filter { it.elementType == PROPERTY }
160→            .mapNotNull { it.findChildByType(IDENTIFIER) }
161→            .any { it.text == identifier }
162→
163→    private companion object {
164→        val LOWER_CAMEL_CASE_REGEXP = "[a-z][a-zA-Z0-9]*".regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters()
165→        val SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE_REGEXP = "[A-Z][_A-Z0-9]*".regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters()
166→        val BACKING_PROPERTY_LOWER_CAMEL_CASE_REGEXP = "_[a-z][a-zA-Z0-9]*".regExIgnoringDiacriticsAndStrokesOnLetters()
167→        const val SERIAL_VERSION_UID_PROPERTY_NAME = "serialVersionUID"
168→
169→        /**
170→         * Mutable types are not considered constant even when declared as `val` because their contents can be modified.
171→         * See: https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide#constant_names
172→         */
173→        val MUTABLE_TYPES =
174→            setOf(
175→                // Kotlin mutable collections
176→                "MutableCollection",
177→                "MutableList",
178→                "MutableSet",
179→                "MutableMap",
180→                "MutableIterator",
181→                "MutableListIterator",
182→                // Common mutable types from Java
183→                "ArrayList",
184→                "LinkedList",
185→                "HashSet",
186→                "LinkedHashSet",
187→                "TreeSet",
188→                "HashMap",
189→                "LinkedHashMap",
190→                "TreeMap",
191→                "Hashtable",
192→                "ArrayDeque",
193→                // Other mutable types
194→                "StringBuilder",
195→                "StringBuffer",
196→            )
197→    }
198→}
199→
200→public val PROPERTY_NAMING_RULE_ID: RuleId = PropertyNamingRule().ruleId
201→
